sections.title: DEFINITION OF PRODUCE
sections.text: This Standard applies to modified stem of commercial varieties of nopals grown from Opuntia ficus indica, O. tomentosa, O. hyptiacantha, O. robusta, O. inermis, O. ondulata , of the Cactaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging. Nopals for industrial processing are excluded.
sections.retrieval_text:
DEFINITION OF PRODUCE
number=1
level=1
This Standard applies to modified stem of commercial varieties of nopals grown from Opuntia ficus indica, O. tomentosa, O. hyptiacantha, O. robusta, O. inermis, O. ondulata , of the Cactaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging. Nopals for industrial processing are excluded.

page_spans.text: This Standard applies to modified stem of commercial varieties of nopals grown from Opuntia ficus indica, O. tomentosa, O. hyptiacantha, O. robusta, O. inermis, O. ondulata , of the Cactaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging. Nopals for industrial processing are excluded.

sections.title: “Extra” Class
sections.text: Nopals in this class must be of superior quality. They must be characteristic of the variety and/or commercial type. They must be free of defects, with the exception of very slight superficial defects, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package.

page_spans.text: Nopals in this class must be of superior quality. They must be characteristic of the variety and/or commercial type. sections.title: Class I
sections.text: Nopals in this class must be of good quality. They must be characteristic of the variety and/or commercial type. The following slight defects, however, may be allowed, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package:

sections.title: PROVISIONS CONCERNING SIZING
sections.text:
Size is determined by the length of the nopal, in accordance with the following table:
Size Code Length (in centimetres)
A 9 - 13
B 13 - 17
C 17 - 21
D 21 - 25
E 25 - 30

sections.title: PROVISIONS CONCERNING TOLERANCES
sections.text: Tolerances in respect of quality and size shall be allowed in each package for produce not satisfying the requirements of the class indicated.

sections.text: For “Extra” Class, 5%; and for Class I or Class II, 10 %; by number or weight of nopals not satisfying the requirements as regards sizing, but falling within the class immediately above or below those indicated in Section 3.

sections.title: Class II
sections.text: Ten percent by number or weight of nopals satisfying neither the requirements of the class nor the minimum requirements, with the exception of produce affected by rotting, pronounced irregularities or any other deterioration rendering it unfit for consumption.

sections.title: PROVISIONS CONCERNING PRESENTATION
sections.retrieval_text:
PROVISIONS CONCERNING PRESENTATION
number=5
level=1

sections.title: MARKING OR LABELLING
sections.retrieval_text:
MARKING OR LABELLING
number=6
level=1

sections.title: UNIFORMITY
sections.text: The contents of each package (or lot for produce presented in bulk) must be uniform and contain only nopals of the same origin, variety, quality and size. For “Extra” Class, colour and ripeness should be uniform. The visible part of the contents of the package (or lot for produce presented in bulk) must be representative of the entire contents.

page_spans.text: The contents of each package (or lot for produce presented in bulk) must be uniform and contain only nopals of the same origin, variety, quality and size. For “Extra” Class, colour and ripeness should be uniform. The visible part of the contents of the package (or lot for produce presented in bulk) must be representative of the entire contents.

sections.title: PACKAGING
sections.text: Nopals must be packed in such a way as to protect the produce properly. The materials used inside the package must be new1, clean, and of a quality such as to avoid causing any external or internal damage to the produce. The use of materials, particularly of paper or stamps bearing trade specifications is allowed, provided the printing or labelling has been done with non-toxic ink or glue. Nopals shall be packed in each container in compliance with the Recommended International Code of Practice for Packaging and Transport of Fresh Fruits and Vegetables (CAC/RCP 44-1995).

sections.title: CONSUMER PACKAGES
sections.text: In addition to the requirements of the Codex General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods (CODEX STAN 1-1985), the following specific provisions apply:

number=6.1
level=2

page_spans.text: In addition to the requirements of the Codex General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods (CODEX STAN 1-1985), the following specific provisions apply:

sections.title: NON-RETAIL CONTAINERS
sections.text: Each package must bear the following particulars, in letters grouped on the same side, legibly and indelibly marked, and visible from the outside, or in the documents accompanying the shipment. For produce transported in bulk these particulars must appear on a document accompanying the goods.

page_spans.text: Each package must bear the following particulars, in letters grouped on the same side, legibly and indelibly marked, and visible from the outside, or in the documents accompanying the shipment. For produce transported in bulk these particulars must appear on a document accompanying the goods.

sections.title: Description of Containers
sections.text: The containers shall meet the quality, hygiene, ventilation and resistance characteristics to ensure suitable handling, shipping and preserving of the nopals. Packages (or lot for produce presented in bulk) must be free of all foreign matter and smell.

sections.title: Nature of Produce
sections.text: If the produce is not visible from the outside, each package shall be labelled as to the name of the produce and may be labelled as to name of the variety.

sentences.original: This Standard applies to modified stem of commercial varieties of nopals grown from Opuntia ficus indica, O. tomentosa, O. hyptiacantha, O. robusta, O. inermis, O. ondulata , of the Cactaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging.

classification.justification: Exclusion of product use is a prohibition and scope limitation but not fitting a known template; new pattern assigned.
notes: Exclusion clauses often appear but current catalog lacks matching template.

classification.function: Specifying mandatory quality criteria and exceptions
classification.pattern: Subject Must Be Qualities List with Exceptions and Conditionals

components.Scope: In all classes, subject to special provisions and tolerances
components.Subject: the nopals
Requirements: sound (excluding rotting or deterioration making unfit)
practically free of visible foreign matter
free of abnormal external moisture (excluding condensation from cold storage removal)
sufficiently developed with satisfactory ripeness depending on nature of produce
components.AdditionalCharacteristic: shape, colour, taste and smell characteristic of the species

classification.justification: The sentence lists detailed mandatory attributes with exceptions and conditions, indicating an obligation. Partial fit with known quality requirement templates, thus adapted template.
notes: Complex list structure but typical of quality specification.

classification.justification: Sentence introduces classification scheme, which is a structural pattern not covered by existing templates; obligation modality applies because classification setup is normative.
notes: Common introductory classification phrase without matching template.

sentences.original:
­ to withstand transport and handling; and
­ to arrive in satisfactory condition at the place of destination.

prefilter.reason: Describes quality criteria related to transportation, relevant to standards.

classification.sentence:
­ to withstand transport and handling; and
­ to arrive in satisfactory condition at the place of destination.
classification.pattern: Quality Objectives Listed as Infinitive Phrases

QualityObjectives: to arrive in satisfactory condition at the place of destination

classification.justification: Lacks explicit subject or predicate; these quality objectives form an obligation but represent a new pattern as such enumerations are not well captured in the known templates.
notes: Fragment style of sentence; interpretable only in context.

sentences.original: Nopals in this class must be of superior quality.

classification.sentence: They must be free of defects, with the exception of very slight superficial defects, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package.
classification.pattern: [Main Obligation] + [Exception introduced by 'with the exception of'] + [Condition introduced by 'provided that']

components.obligation: They must be free of defects
components.exception: with the exception of very slight superficial defects
components.condition: provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package

classification.justification: The sentence expresses a normative obligation to be defect-free but includes a specific exception and a condition qualifying that exception. This composite structure with both exception and conditional qualification does not strongly match any known templates, leading to a new pattern classification with high confidence in modality and function.
notes: The sentence combines an obligation with both an exception and a condition, which is a complex normative structure absent from the current template catalog.

sentences.original: Tolerances in respect of quality and size shall be allowed in each package for produce not satisfying the requirements of the class indicated.

classification.sentence: Tolerances in respect of quality and size shall be allowed in each package for produce not satisfying the requirements of the class indicated.
classification.pattern: [Subject] + shall be allowed + [Scope] + for [Condition]

components.subject: Tolerances in respect of quality and size
components.condition: for produce not satisfying the requirements of the class indicated

classification.justification: The sentence grants permission (shall be allowed) regarding tolerances with a specific scope and conditional clause, but no exact matching template exists. The pattern combines permission with conditional allowance tied to product quality and packaging context, representing a new pattern.
notes: The sentence uses 'shall be allowed' indicating formal permission, but linked to a conditional scope rather than a simple permission; this may overlap with obligation or condition categories but primarily expresses permission.

sentences.original: If the produce is not visible from the outside, each package shall be labelled as to the name of the produce and may be labelled as to name of the variety.

classification.sentence: If the produce is not visible from the outside, each package shall be labelled as to the name of the produce and may be labelled as to name of the variety.
classification.modality: Condition for the first clause; Obligation and Permission for the second clause
classification.function: Conditional obligation combined with conditional permission
classification.pattern: If [Condition], [Obligation] and [Permission] on the subject applies

components.Condition: If the produce is not visible from the outside
components.Obligation: each package shall be labelled as to the name of the produce
components.Permission: each package may be labelled as to name of the variety

classification.justification: The sentence has a conditional antecedent followed by two coordinated modal expressions: obligation and permission. This combination with conditional modality and mixed normative statements does not strongly match known templates and requires a new structural pattern to capture the conditional plus dual modality coordination.
notes: The sentence blends condition, obligation, and permission within a single coordinated consequent, which is less common in typical regulatory templates and thus lacks close template match.

classification.sentence: Country of origin and, optionally, district where grown or national, regional or local place name.

components.optional_component: district where grown or national, regional or local place name

classification.justification: The sentence provides optional information attributes related to place names without imposing obligations, conditions, prohibitions, or limits, thus fitting a Permission modality with an informational communicative function. It does not match any known templates; its structure is a main subject with an optional extension listing place-related descriptors.
notes: The sentence is fragmentary and lacks a verb, which limits clarity on its exact communicative function. It appears to allow optional inclusion of certain details but does not explicitly state permission in a normative form.

pages.title: STANDARD FOR NOPAL (CODEX STAN 185-1993)
pages.summary: Página apresenta o documento CODEX STAN 185-1993 para Nopal, com seções principais e subseções numeradas até nível 3. A seção 2 e suas subseções indicam conteúdo que continua para a próxima página, especialmente na classificação das classes.

pages.title: CODEX STAN 185
pages.summary: A página apresenta duas seções principais: seção 3 sobre 'PROVISIONS CONCERNING SIZING' e seção 4 sobre 'PROVISIONS CONCERNING TOLERANCES'. A seção 4 contém subseções 4.1 (com subdivisões 4.1.1, 4.1.2 e 4.1.3) e 4.2. O texto está claramente associado aos títulos numerados, sem indicação de continuidade entre páginas para essas seções nesta página.

pages.title: CODEX STAN 185
pages.summary: A página contém as seções principais 5 e 6, cada uma com suas subseções enumeradas. Os textos estão claramente associados aos respectivos títulos, sem indicação de continuidade entre páginas. O cabeçalho da página é "CODEX STAN 185 Page 3 of 4" e o título principal do documento é "CODEX STAN 185".

pages.title: CODEX STAN 185
pages.summary: A página 4 mantém a continuidade das subseções 6.2.4 e 6.2.5 iniciadas na página anterior, com títulos visíveis. As seções 7 CONTAMINANTS e 8 HYGIENE são claramente identificadas com suas subseções enumeradas, sem texto interno detalhado, mas diretamente associadas aos seus títulos. Não há indicação de continuidade para a próxima página.
